race.name: Sapporo Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers through Hokkaido's deep powder country — Japan's Olympic city delivers world-class snow and meticulous organization.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple service stations with Japanese refreshments

climate.primary: Heavy snowfall maritime (Sea of Japan effect)
climate.description: Sapporo receives some of the heaviest snowfall of any major city on Earth thanks to cold Siberian air crossing the Sea of Japan. February temperatures range from -10°C to -2°C. Snow depth regularly exceeds 1 meter in the city, with deeper accumulation in surrounding hills. The snow is drier and lighter than typical maritime snow.
challenges: Heavy fresh snowfall can soften tracks mid-race
Deep powder alongside groomed trails if you fall
Cold Siberian air mass brings sudden temperature drops
Humidity from Sea of Japan effect affects waxing

course.primary: Loop course through the forested parkland surrounding Sapporo, using trail infrastructure built for the 1972 Winter Olympics. Gently rolling terrain with short climbs through birch and conifer forest.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through Olympic-legacy parkland
course.grooming: Japanese precision grooming — meticulous trail preparation
features: Set in Sapporo, host city of the 1972 Winter Olympics
Hokkaido's legendary snow depth guarantees conditions
Japanese organizational precision — timing, logistics, hospitality
Gateway to Sapporo Snow Festival (early February)

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Perfect snow reliability from Hokkaido's legendary snowfall anchors the score. Japanese organizational standards deliver a polished experience. Olympic city accessibility is excellent. Moderate competitive depth reflects predominantly domestic field.

history.summary: Founded in 1981, the Sapporo Ski Marathon capitalizes on the cross-country skiing infrastructure built for the 1972 Winter Olympics. Sapporo, one of the snowiest cities on Earth, provides unmatched natural snow conditions. The race has become Japan's premier cross-country ski marathon and a Worldloppet member, attracting both Japanese recreational skiers and international Worldloppet passport collectors drawn by the unique combination of Japanese culture and Hokkaido powder.
notable_facts: Built on infrastructure from the 1972 Sapporo Winter Olympics
Sapporo receives over 5 meters of snowfall annually — among the snowiest cities on Earth
Worldloppet member event, the only one in Japan
Coincides with the famous Sapporo Snow Festival in early February
Showcases Japan's unique cross-country skiing culture on Hokkaido

videos.title: Philipp the Race Reporter at the Sapporo Skimarathon 2019
videos.description:
The Worldloppet Race Reporter Philipp travelled to Japan, to the Sapporo Skimarathon, to check the race out for you and to collect a stamp in his passport! 
Check out Philipps experience in Japan!

quotes.text: The weather is really nice the tracks are nice I'm really looking forward... and the famous Sapporo Dome finish line venue that fits 50,000 people and was built for football championships and later used in the Nordic World Championships 2007.

The tracks are in really good condition here at the finish line and there were people who speak English so no troubles picking my bib up, and there are stables and wax benches where you can fix your skis yourself.
